meta.name: Alex Rivera
meta.tagline: I build systems that scale quietly.

hero.subtext: Full-stack developer specializing in scalable backend architecture and interfaces that don't get in the way.

journey.title: First Line of Code
journey.description: Started with HTML/CSS, broke things, kept going.

journey.title: First Freelance Client
journey.description: Built a booking system for a local business. Got paid. Got hooked.

journey.title: Joined a Startup
journey.description: Learned what 'production' actually means under real users.

journey.title: Went Full-Stack Architect
journey.description: Stopped just writing features. Started designing systems.

journey.title: Independent Practice
journey.description: Now building for a small portfolio of serious clients.

experience.description: Leading backend architecture for a multi-tenant SaaS platform serving 200+ organizations.
Owned product end-to-end for a field-operations tool used by logistics teams.
Built websites and small tools for local businesses and startups.

projects.title: Ledger
projects.subtitle: Real-time financial reconciliation platform
projects.description: A multi-tenant system that reconciles transactions across 12 payment providers in real time, replacing a 6-hour nightly batch job.

projects.title: Fieldnote
projects.subtitle: Offline-first field operations tool
projects.description: Lets logistics teams log inspections without signal and sync automatically when back online. Used daily by 800+ field agents.

projects.title: Arc
projects.subtitle: Internal design system & component library
projects.description: A token-driven design system adopted across 5 product teams, cutting new-feature build time by a third.

projects.title: Pulse
projects.subtitle: Observability dashboard for microservices
projects.description: Self-hosted alternative to Datadog for a 30-service architecture — built for a fifth of the cost.

howIWork.title: Understand the real problem
howIWork.description: Most requests aren't the actual problem. I ask until we find the one worth solving.

howIWork.title: Design the system, not the screen
howIWork.description: Interfaces are the last 10%. The architecture underneath is what determines if it scales.

howIWork.title: Ship small, ship often
howIWork.description: Working software in front of real users beats a perfect plan on a whiteboard.

howIWork.title: Leave it better documented
howIWork.description: Whoever inherits this code — including future me — should never have to guess why.

principles.title: Simplicity is a feature
principles.description: Every dependency, abstraction, and clever trick is a future liability. I default to boring technology.

principles.title: Slow is smooth, smooth is fast
principles.description: Rushing architecture decisions costs 10x more time later. I take the time upfront.

principles.title: Code is read more than written
principles.description: I optimize for the next person reading this — who is usually me, six months from now.

principles.title: Defaults matter
principles.description: The right default setting prevents more bugs than the cleverest error handling.

testimonials.quote: Rebuilt our core data pipeline in three weeks. It hadn't been touched in two years because everyone was scared of it.
testimonials.author: Priya N.

testimonials.quote: The kind of engineer who tells you the feature is a bad idea before you waste a sprint on it.
testimonials.author: Marcus T.

contact.headline: Let's build something that lasts.
contact.subtext: Currently taking on a small number of new projects per quarter. If the problem is interesting, I'm listening.
